pr.title: [codex] Add not-so-yolo CLI mode
pr.body:
## Summary

Adds a shared `--not-so-yolo` CLI flag for interactive and exec entry points. The flag runs Codex with workspace-write sandboxing, on-request approvals, and auto-review as the approvals reviewer, giving a less-permissive alternative to `--yolo`.

The change keeps the behavior consistent with existing permission-mode handling: the flag conflicts with `--yolo`, conflicts with explicit approval-policy flags where applicable, inherits from the top-level `codex --not-so-yolo exec ...` form, and is documented in `codex-rs/README.md`.

## Tests

- `just fmt`
- `cargo check -p codex-utils-cli -p codex-cli -p codex-exec -p codex-tui`
- `cargo test -p codex-utils-cli`
- `cargo test -p codex-cli -p codex-exec`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=8388608 cargo test -p codex-tui`
- `git diff --check`

Note: plain `cargo test -p codex-tui` stack-overflows in the existing `app::tests::attach_live_thread_for_selection_rejects_unmaterialized_fallback_threads`; that same test and the full TUI suite pass with `RUST_MIN_STACK=8388608`.
pr.author: jgershen-oai
